questions.subject: History
questions.prompt: Regarding the historical figure Major Dhyan Chand, after whom India's highest sporting award is named, which of the following is true?

options.text: He was offered German citizenship by Adolf Hitler during the 1936 Berlin Olympics.
He scored over 100 goals in just 12 Olympic matches.
The Khel Ratna award has carried his name since its inception in 1991.
He is the only sportsperson to have received the Laureus Lifetime Achievement award.

questions.explanation: Option A is correct: During the 1936 Berlin Games, Hitler offered him German citizenship. Option B is incorrect: He scored 33 goals in 12 Olympic matches. Option C is incorrect: It was renamed in 2021; it was originally the Rajiv Gandhi Khel Ratna. Option D is not supported by the text.
questions.takeaway: Major Dhyan Chand's legendary status is highlighted by his Olympic record and the 1936 Berlin Games anecdote.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: During the 1936 Berlin Games, German Chancellor Adolf Hitler was so enamoured with Dhyan Chand’s play that he offered him German citizenship... Dhyan Chand played 12 Olympic matches, scoring 33 goals.
